title: Atirmociclib Phase 2 Data Support Pfizer Oncology Pipeline
text: Pfizer announced positive topline Phase 2 results for atirmociclib, an investigational next-generation CDK4 inhibitor, in second-line hormone receptor-positive, HER2-negative advanced or metastatic breast cancer. The randomized FOURLIGHT-1 study met its primary endpoint, showing a statistically significant and clinically meaningful improvement in progression-free survival with atirmociclib plus fulvestrant compared with fulvestrant or everolimus plus exemestane. Pfizer reported a 40% reduction in the risk of disease progression or death and said more than 90% of patients started atirmociclib within three months of prior CDK4/6 inhibitor therapy. The safety profile was manageable, with 6.4% of patients discontinuing atirmociclib because of treatment-emergent adverse events. This news is useful evidence because oncology is central to Pfizer's post-COVID growth strategy, and positive data for a potential first-in-class CDK4 inhibitor could strengthen Pfizer's late-stage pipeline. The risk is that atirmociclib remains investigational and still requires larger studies, regulatory review, and successful commercialization.
